title_original: Qoro Launches Solo: A Cloud Platform for Parallelized Quantum Simulation - Quantum Computing Report
summary_original: Qoro Quantum has launched Solo, a cloud platform enabling parallelized simulation of hybrid quantum-classical workloads. In internal tests, Solo completed a 9,000-job Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm max-cut workload in under 10 minutes, compared to over 24 hours on conventional setups. The platform integrates Divi (an open-source workload construction library), Composer (orchestration for heterogeneous hardware), and Maestro (an intelligent simulation framework), treating CPUs, GPUs, and quantum processors as one logical system. This “Software as a Service” approach aims to make quantum development easier, reducing the need for deep expertise or custom integrations. Led by CEO Dan Holme and CTO Dr. Stephen DiAdamo, Qoro Quantum targets enterprise-scale problems in finance, logistics, and materials science that surpass classical computing but remain out of reach for current quantum hardware.
